texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Part 1: Fresh Mac Mini Build — Three Options
texts.text: Part 1: Fresh Mac Mini Build — Three Options

texts.label: text
texts.orig: You have a working Tier 2 RAG system on your current Mac Mini. To deploy it on a new Mac Mini, there are three approaches, each with different tradeoffs.
texts.text: You have a working Tier 2 RAG system on your current Mac Mini. To deploy it on a new Mac Mini, there are three approaches, each with different tradeoffs.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Copy your scripts and config to the new machine, run the automated setup. This is the cleanest approach because it creates a fresh environment with no accumulated cruft from the build process.
texts.text: Copy your scripts and config to the new machine, run the automated setup. This is the cleanest approach because it creates a fresh environment with no accumulated cruft from the build process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 45–60 minutes (plus 30–60 min for indexing with contextual retrieval)
texts.text: 45–60 minutes (plus 30–60 min for indexing with contextual retrieval)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Low — follow the checklist step by step
texts.text: Low — follow the checklist step by step

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The checklist below reflects the Tier 2 system including Ollama, nomic-embed-text, hybrid search, and contextual retrieval.
texts.text: The checklist below reflects the Tier 2 system including Ollama, nomic-embed-text, hybrid search, and contextual retrieval.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: brew install ollama && brew services start ollama
texts.text: brew install ollama && brew services start ollama

texts.label: text
texts.orig: pip install chromadb anthropic tiktoken python-docx openpyxl python-pptx pdfplumber rank_bm25 numpy requests
texts.text: pip install chromadb anthropic tiktoken python-docx openpyxl python-pptx pdfplumber rank_bm25 numpy requests

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 12. Export conversations from Claude.ai, run import + index + summarize:
texts.text: 12. Export conversations from Claude.ai, run import + index + summarize:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Copy .plist files to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and load them
texts.text: Copy .plist files to ~/Library/LaunchAgents/ and load them

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 16. Install OpenClaw, configure SOUL.md, BOOT.md, and the RAG skill
texts.text: 16. Install OpenClaw, configure SOUL.md, BOOT.md, and the RAG skill

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Option B: Full System Clone via rsync
texts.text: Option B: Full System Clone via rsync

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Copy the entire ~/claude-memory/ directory and ~/.openclaw/ directory to the new machine. Fastest approach but carries over any artifacts from the build process.
texts.text: Copy the entire ~/claude-memory/ directory and ~/.openclaw/ directory to the new machine. Fastest approach but carries over any artifacts from the build process.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Old cached files, stale .pyc, or broken symlinks may carry over
texts.text: Old cached files, stale .pyc, or broken symlinks may carry over

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Steps: Install Homebrew, Python 3.12, Ollama, and pull nomic-embed-text on the new machine. Then rsync -av ~/claude-memory/ newmac:~/claude-memory/ and rsync the OpenClaw config. Create the venv on the new machine, install packages, and test. The vectordb transfers intact — no re-indexing needed.
texts.text: Steps: Install Homebrew, Python 3.12, Ollama, and pull nomic-embed-text on the new machine. Then rsync -av ~/claude-memory/ newmac:~/claude-memory/ and rsync the OpenClaw config. Create the venv on the new machine, install packages, and test. The vectordb transfers intact — no re-indexing needed.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Create a single bash script that does everything in Option A automatically. This is the best approach if you plan to deploy on multiple machines or want a repeatable, hands-off installation.
texts.text: Create a single bash script that does everything in Option A automatically. This is the best approach if you plan to deploy on multiple machines or want a repeatable, hands-off installation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 2–3 hours to write and test the script, then 5 minutes per machine
texts.text: 2–3 hours to write and test the script, then 5 minutes per machine

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Medium to write, trivial to run
texts.text: Medium to write, trivial to run

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Multiple deployments, client installations, or selling the system
texts.text: Multiple deployments, client installations, or selling the system

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The script would install all dependencies, create the folder structure, copy scripts from a USB drive or cloud location, configure the API key (prompted), set up launchd watchers, and run the initial index. This is the foundation for the commercialization options below.
texts.text: The script would install all dependencies, create the folder structure, copy scripts from a USB drive or cloud location, configure the API key (prompted), set up launchd watchers, and run the initial index. This is the foundation for the commercialization options below.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Part 2: Packaging the RAG System for Sale
texts.text: Part 2: Packaging the RAG System for Sale

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Your system solves a real problem: giving small businesses and professionals persistent AI memory that runs locally, stays private, and costs almost nothing to operate. There are several ways to commercialize this, from simple to complex.
texts.text: Your system solves a real problem: giving small businesses and professionals persistent AI memory that runs locally, stays private, and costs almost nothing to operate. There are several ways to commercialize this, from simple to complex.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sell the installation as a consulting service. You show up (physically or remotely), set up the Mac Mini, configure everything, train the client on how to drop files into the RAG Inbox and use Telegram. Charge a flat setup fee plus optional monthly support.
texts.text: Sell the installation as a consulting service. You show up (physically or remotely), set up the Mac Mini, configure everything, train the client on how to drop files into the RAG Inbox and use Telegram. Charge a flat setup fee plus optional monthly support.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is the fastest path to revenue. You already know how to do the install. The MacDailyNews article about Mac Mini AI agents confirms there’s a growing market of professionals buying Mac Minis specifically for local AI setups. You’d be selling the expertise to make it work.
texts.text: This is the fastest path to revenue. You already know how to do the install. The MacDailyNews article about Mac Mini AI agents confirms there’s a growing market of professionals buying Mac Minis specifically for local AI setups. You’d be selling the expertise to make it work.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Create a polished install script (or macOS .pkg installer) that a technically-comfortable user can run themselves. Package it with documentation, a quick-start guide, and email support. Sell as a digital download.
texts.text: Create a polished install script (or macOS .pkg installer) that a technically-comfortable user can run themselves. Package it with documentation, a quick-start guide, and email support. Sell as a digital download.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The installer would be a bash script that checks prerequisites, installs dependencies, creates the folder structure, deploys your scripts, and walks the user through API key setup and first indexing. macOS also supports .pkg installers for more polished distribution. You’d sell it through Gumroad or similar, with Stripe for payments.
texts.text: The installer would be a bash script that checks prerequisites, installs dependencies, creates the folder structure, deploys your scripts, and walks the user through API key setup and first indexing. macOS also supports .pkg installers for more polished distribution. You’d sell it through Gumroad or similar, with Stripe for payments.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Option 3: Turnkey Mac Mini + Software Bundle (Highest Revenue)
texts.text: Option 3: Turnkey Mac Mini + Software Bundle (Highest Revenue)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Buy Mac Minis wholesale (or have the client buy one), pre-install everything, and ship a ready-to-use unit. The client plugs it in, connects to WiFi, opens Telegram, and starts asking questions. Zero setup required on their end.
texts.text: Buy Mac Minis wholesale (or have the client buy one), pre-install everything, and ship a ready-to-use unit. The client plugs it in, connects to WiFi, opens Telegram, and starts asking questions. Zero setup required on their end.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is the “Jarvis in a box” model that MacDailyNews described. The market is real — people are already buying Mac Minis for this purpose but struggling with the setup. You’d be selling the last mile.
texts.text: This is the “Jarvis in a box” model that MacDailyNews described. The market is real — people are already buying Mac Minis for this purpose but struggling with the setup. You’d be selling the last mile.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Option 4: SaaS / Hosted RAG (Most Scalable, Most Complex)
texts.text: Option 4: SaaS / Hosted RAG (Most Scalable, Most Complex)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Instead of shipping hardware, host the RAG system in the cloud and sell access as a subscription. Each client gets their own isolated ChromaDB instance, their own document pipeline, and access via a web UI or Telegram.
texts.text: Instead of shipping hardware, host the RAG system in the cloud and sell access as a subscription. Each client gets their own isolated ChromaDB instance, their own document pipeline, and access via a web UI or Telegram.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This contradicts one of the system’s core selling points (privacy, fully local). But for clients who don’t want to manage hardware, it’s a valid model. You’d need multi-tenant isolation, authentication, and a web interface.
texts.text: This contradicts one of the system’s core selling points (privacy, fully local). But for clients who don’t want to manage hardware, it’s a valid model. You’d need multi-tenant isolation, authentication, and a web interface.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: What You Can and Cannot Sell
texts.text: What You Can and Cannot Sell

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 100% yours. You wrote them (with Claude’s help). Fully sellable.
texts.text: 100% yours. You wrote them (with Claude’s help). Fully sellable.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Apache 2.0 license. Free for commercial use.
texts.text: Apache 2.0 license. Free for commercial use.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: MIT license. Free for commercial use.
texts.text: MIT license. Free for commercial use.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Apache 2.0 license. Free for commercial use.
texts.text: Apache 2.0 license. Free for commercial use.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Check the license. It’s open source but you’d need to verify commercial use terms.
texts.text: Check the license. It’s open source but you’d need to verify commercial use terms.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each client needs their own API key. You cannot resell API access. Anthropic’s terms require each user to have their own account.
texts.text: Each client needs their own API key. You cannot resell API access. Anthropic’s terms require each user to have their own account.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All MIT or Apache licensed. No restrictions.
texts.text: All MIT or Apache licensed. No restrictions.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Start with Option 1 (Done-For-You Service) immediately. It requires zero additional development. Take on 3–5 clients, charge $1,000–$1,500 per setup plus $100/month support. Use those engagements to refine the install process and identify what clients struggle with.
texts.text: Start with Option 1 (Done-For-You Service) immediately. It requires zero additional development. Take on 3–5 clients, charge $1,000–$1,500 per setup plus $100/month support. Use those engagements to refine the install process and identify what clients struggle with.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In parallel, develop the Option 2 automated installer. Every time you do a manual install, document what you did and automate another step. After 5 installs, your installer script will cover 90% of cases. Then sell the script as a digital product for passive income alongside the service offering.
texts.text: In parallel, develop the Option 2 automated installer. Every time you do a manual install, document what you did and automate another step. After 5 installs, your installer script will cover 90% of cases. Then sell the script as a digital product for passive income alongside the service offering.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Option 3 (Turnkey Mac Mini) is the eventual product if demand justifies it. But don’t invest in hardware inventory until you have paying clients proving the market.
texts.text: Option 3 (Turnkey Mac Mini) is the eventual product if demand justifies it. But don’t invest in hardware inventory until you have paying clients proving the market.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The MacDailyNews article from February 2026 confirms that Mac Mini AI agent setups are a real and growing trend. People are buying hardware specifically for this use case but lack the technical ability to configure it. Your system is ahead of most — it has hybrid search, contextual retrieval, and a polished automation pipeline that most DIY setups lack.
texts.text: The MacDailyNews article from February 2026 confirms that Mac Mini AI agent setups are a real and growing trend. People are buying hardware specifically for this use case but lack the technical ability to configure it. Your system is ahead of most — it has hybrid search, contextual retrieval, and a polished automation pipeline that most DIY setups lack.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Potential positioning: “Personal AI memory for professionals. Your conversations, documents, and business knowledge — searchable, private, and always available. Runs on a Mac Mini in your office. No cloud. No subscription. Your data stays yours.”
texts.text: Potential positioning: “Personal AI memory for professionals. Your conversations, documents, and business knowledge — searchable, private, and always available. Runs on a Mac Mini in your office. No cloud. No subscription. Your data stays yours.”

texts.label: text

Loses the privacy-first local advantage. Significant infrastructure work. Ongoing hosting costs.
